note on sources: there is famously no UGCC office to Methodius, so common one from Stanford Molytvoslov is used here
ps140: Wholly consecrated,* you have appeared as a Godbearer.* As holy chrism of God, you are clothed with the Holy Spirit.* You have always entered serenely into the holy of holies,* and are illumined with the splendor proceeding from God,* and you partake of the holy mysteries of grace,* as a true, and most glorious bishop.** With boldness, intercede for our souls.

Your life was resplendent with the radiance of virtue.* O blessed bishop Methodius;* you truly appeared as a bright sun,* thus you illumined the faithful and dispersed the darkness of deceit*. Having become, by the grace of the Holy Spirit, a son of day,* you now live where the never- setting light shines.* Therefore, we honorably celebrate your divine and light-bearing memory.** O ever-memorable one, we lovingly venerate you.

Your mind has become deified,* for it is directed toward God and nourished by faith.* You are wise in the ways of God.* You learned about incorruption in a mortal and corrupt body, O all-glorious one!* You have acquired the serenity of the angels.* Although you contain passions within you, you adorned yourself with impassivity,* O father Methodius, wisest of bishops,** you are a bright light and intercessor for those who lovingly keep your memory.

The fruit of your virtues, O venerable father,* has enlightened the hearts of the faithful;* for who, hearing of your immeasurable humility,* would not wonder at your patience,* at your gentleness towards the needy,* at your consolation of the sorrowing?* You have divinely instructed all, O bishop Methodius,* and now you are adorned with the never-fading crown.** Therefore, we beg you to pray for us.
aposticha: O bishop Methodius, star of divine light, resplendent lamp of the Church,* ornament of the hierarchs,* true model of piety, our intercessor,** save our souls from the destroying enemy.
O bishop Methodius,* you received from God great power* to repel raging demons by your prayers.** Therefore, from their malice protect all who hasten to you.

How can we fittingly honor you, O blessed bishop Methodius,* righteous and divine light that enlightens all with divine doctrine* and reveals to all deep mysteries.* With one mind we cry out:** Rejoice, O father and hierarch Methodius!

Angels and men marveled at the life you led in the body,* which resembled that of the angels.* Priests and monks devoutly venerated it,* kings and princes held it in high esteem, and God glorified it.* Implore Christ, O priestly father,** to have mercy on us who honor you.
aposticha_verses: Precious in the eyes of the Lord* is the death of His faithful.
How can I repay the Lord,* for His goodness to me?
